title: Bridge Essay: Exploring Habits in Anorexia Nervosa: Promise, Pitfalls, and Progress.
concept: The mechanical mind — neuroscience of automaticity revealing how we become trapped in behavioral loops.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** The mechanical mind — neuroscience of automaticity revealing how we become trapped in behavioral loops.

**Exploring Habits in Anorexia Nervosa: Promise, Pitfalls, and Progress.** - Current psychiatry reports (2025). DOI: 10.1007/s11920-025-01588-7. PMID: 40016535. PURPOSE OF REVIEW: Habits, characterized by automaticity and insensitivity to outcomes, may be key to the persistence of maladaptive behaviors in anorexia nervosa (AN). This review examines the status of habit research in AN, focusing on insights from task-based assessments. Habits, characterized by automaticity and insensitivity to outcomes, may be key to the persistence of maladaptive behaviors in anorexia nervosa (AN).
